_comment: v10 Phase C.1 worked-example registry (spec-v10 §5.1). One or more rows per tile recording a worked example from a primary public source: tile id, source citation, inputs, expected outputs with tolerance. The unit-test runner (when wired in Phase C migration) reads the registry and asserts each tile's output matches within tolerance. This file is the migration target for the existing per-test fixture obligation; tiles are added incrementally.
_schema: Each row: { tile_id, source_publisher, source_title, source_edition_or_year, source_section_or_page, verified_by, verified_on (ISO date), inputs (object), outputs (object: { name: { value, tolerance: { abs?, pct? } } }) }. Tolerance defaults per spec-v10 §5.3: code-table 1 percent or 1 unit-of-least-precision; first-principles 0.5 percent; standard-rounded sizes exact match.
_coverage_target: Spec-v10 §5.2 declares this file the migration target. Every tile must eventually have at least one row. Current coverage is a starter set; new tiles ship a row with their PR per the contributor checklist. The check-worked-examples.mjs linter reports coverage but does not (yet) fail CI on missing rows. Fail-on-missing is enabled when coverage exceeds 80 percent.

rows.source_section_or_page: 4:1 rig (3 pulleys), efficiency 0.9, 600 lb load -> theoretical MA 4 / actual MA 2.916 / haul force 205.76 lb

Rectangle area identity: A = L * W

rows.source_title: SEER ~ EER * 1.12; SEER2 ~ SEER * 0.95 (averaged across rating conditions)

rows.source_section_or_page: r=10 ft / sector 90 deg -> circumference 62.832 / area 314.159 / sector 78.540

rows.source_title: Hydrostatic test pressure = working pressure * multiplier (1.5x water / 1.25x fuel gas); hold time piecewise by system volume

rows.source_title: Voltage imbalance percent = (max deviation from average) / average * 100; HP derate per NEMA MG-1 table; derate factor = 1 - 2*(imb/100)^2

rows.source_title: Tile count = ceil(area / tile_face) + ceil(base * waste); grout joints based on tile perimeter

rows.source_title: Roofing squares = (area / 100) * (1 + waste); architectural shingles 3 bundles/square; underlayment 4 squares/roll; waste scales with pitch
rows.source_section_or_page: 2200 ft^2 roof / 6:12 pitch (12% waste) / architectural shingles / 200 ft perimeter -> 24.64 squares / 74 bundles / 7 underlayment rolls / 200 lf drip edge & starter

rows.source_title: Power-factor correction: kVAR = kW * (tan(acos(pf1)) - tan(acos(pf2))); per-leg capacitance from Q = V^2 * 2*pi*f*C
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; standard PF correction identity at 60 Hz
rows.source_section_or_page: 100 kW / pf 0.75 -> 0.95 / 480 V three-phase -> 55.32 kVAR / 636.93 uF per leg (V_LN = 480/sqrt(3))

rows.source_title: Simply-supported uniform-load deflection: delta = 5*w*L^4 / (384*E*I); span limits L/360 and L/240
rows.source_section_or_page: 50 plf / 12 ft span / E 1.6e6 psi / I 47.6 in^4 (typical 2x10 DF#2) -> 0.306 in deflection vs 0.400 in L/360 limit -> PASS

rows.source_title: CCC = DIO + DSO - DPO (standard working-capital identity)
rows.source_section_or_page: DSO 45 / DIO 60 / DPO 30 -> CCC 75 days

rows.source_title: Bond-strength embedment: ld = T / (0.7 * sqrt(fc) * pi * d) (uplift T_lb, diameter d_in, concrete strength fc_psi)
rows.source_section_or_page: 5000 lb uplift / 5/8 in bolt / 3000 psi concrete -> 66.42 in embedment / 5.53 ft (governing design will use a larger factored capacity; this is the screening formula)

rows.source_title: Inventory turnover = COGS / average inventory; DSI = period_days / turnover (standard accounting ratio)
rows.source_section_or_page: $2,000,000 COGS / $250k beginning / $270k ending / 365 days -> turnover 7.69x / DSI 47.45 days / avg inv $260,000

rows.source_title: Drainage slope: in_per_ft = (rise/run)*12; percent = (in_per_ft/12)*100; degrees = atan(in_per_ft/12)
rows.source_section_or_page: Rise 1, run 4 (same units) -> 3 in/ft / 25% / 14.04 deg / fraction 0.25 (typical short-run pitch)

rows.source_title: Bar count along each axis = floor((dim_in - 2*edge)/spacing) + 1; total length = bars_w*(width-2*edge) + bars_l*(length-2*edge)
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; standard slab-on-grade rebar grid layout
rows.source_section_or_page: 20 ft x 10 ft slab / 12 in spacing / 3 in edge clearance / #4 -> 10 bars long axis / 20 bars short axis / 385 lf total

rows.source_title: units = ceil((area / coverage_per_unit) * (1 + waste_factor)); drywall 4x8 sheet covers 32 ft^2 with 10% waste
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; industry coverage rules of thumb
rows.source_section_or_page: 1000 ft^2 / drywall 4x8 (32 ft^2 per sheet, 10% waste) -> 31.25 raw / 35 sheets with waste

rows.source_title: Units per ft^2 = 144 / ((w + joint)*(h + joint)); add ceil(base * waste_factor)
rows.source_section_or_page: 100 ft^2 wall / CMU 8x8x16 / 3/8 in joints / 5% waste -> face 0.952 ft^2 / base 106 / 112 units with waste

rows.source_title: tons = volume_yd3 * 27 ft^3/yd^3 * pcf / 2000; wood-frame debris ~ 50 pcf bulk
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; industry haul-weight rules of thumb
rows.source_section_or_page: Wood-frame demo / 25 yd^3 -> 675 ft^3 / 16.875 tons / 30 yd^3 dumpster (smallest >= 25)

rows.source_title: Nail withdrawal: W (lb/in penetration) = G^2.5 * D * 1380, where G is wood specific gravity and D is shank diameter; screw factor ~1.85x
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; public AWC/NDS withdrawal identity (G^2.5 * D * 1380)
rows.source_section_or_page: 16d common nail (D=0.162 in) in DF-L (G=0.50), 1.5 in penetration -> 39.52 lb/in / 59.28 lb total

rows.source_title: Transformer kVA = load_kW / power_factor
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; arithmetic identity over ANSI/IEEE C57 standard kVA steps (15, 30, 45, 75, 112.5, 150, 225, 300, 500, 750, 1000)
rows.source_section_or_page: 90 kW @ 0.9 pf -> 100 kVA required; next ANSI standard step is 112.5 kVA

rows.source_title: NEC 2023 Table 310.16 ampacity (75 C column)
rows.source_section_or_page: 12 AWG copper THWN/THHN at 30 C ambient, single conductor; project bundles a physics-derived value (24.4 A) under the table's 25 A published cap.

rows.source_title: Hazen-Williams head loss: hf = 0.2083 * (100/C)^1.852 * Q^1.852 / d^4.8655 (ft per 100 ft)
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; classical Hazen-Williams identity (PVC C=150, SCH40 ID for 1 in = 1.049 in)
rows.source_section_or_page: 10 gpm through 100 ft of 1 in SCH40 PVC -> ~2.38 ft head / ~1.03 psi at ~3.7 ft/s velocity (within typical)

rows.source_title: Spitzglass low-pressure gas flow: Q = 3550 * sqrt(d^5 * dP / (SG * L))
rows.source_edition_or_year: NFPA 54 (National Fuel Gas Code) low-pressure derivation; natural gas heating value 1030 BTU/ft^3, SG 0.60
rows.source_section_or_page: 100,000 BTU natural gas at 50 ft / 0.5 in w.c. drop -> 97.09 cfh required; 1/2 in SCH40 passes (139.84 cfh capacity)

rows.source_title: Refrigerant superheat = line_temperature_F - saturated_temperature_F at suction pressure
rows.source_section_or_page: R-410A at 118 psig saturates at ~40 F; suction line at 50 F yields 10 F superheat (in the 5-25 F TXV target band)

rows.source_title: Lighting power density (LPD) target: office class ~1.0 W/ft^2
rows.source_edition_or_year: IECC 2021 / ASHRAE 90.1-2019 (allowance varies by code edition; project bundles a representative office allowance)
rows.source_section_or_page: 1000 ft^2 office @ 1.0 W/ft^2 -> 1000 W target lighting load

rows.source_title: Low-voltage DC voltage drop on a 2-wire run: V_drop = 2 * I * R_per_ft * L
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; physical resistance of 10 AWG Cu at 25 C ~ 1.018 milliohm/ft (NIST); LED driver tolerance band ~3%
rows.source_section_or_page: 12 V / 10 AWG Cu / 20 ft / 10 A LED lighting -> ~0.407 V drop (~3.4%); above the 3% LED-driver band, so 'not acceptable'

rows.source_title: Condenser approach = condenser saturation_F - outdoor_F; cooling delta-T = return_F - supply_F
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; ACCA Manual S / typical commissioning ranges (approach 8-20 F nominal; cooling DT 15-22 F)
rows.source_section_or_page: Outdoor 90 F / condenser sat 105 F -> approach 15 F (normal). Return 75 F / supply 55 F -> delta-T 20 F (normal).

rows.source_title: Mass-weighted mixing of return + outdoor airstreams at a given outdoor-air fraction
rows.source_section_or_page: Return 75 F / 50% RH, outdoor 95 F / 60% RH, OA fraction 0.20 -> mixed dry-bulb 79 F (0.8 * 75 + 0.2 * 95).

rows.source_title: Manning's equation for partially-full pipe flow: V = (1.486/n) * R^(2/3) * S^(1/2)
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; classical Manning identity (PVC roughness n = 0.009; half-full geometry)
rows.source_section_or_page: 4 in PVC sewer at 50 gpm target -> slope ~0.0788 in/ft (slope_for_flow), self-cleansing slope ~0.0484 in/ft

rows.source_title: PDI WH-201: Water Hammer Arresters - Sizing by Total Branch WSFU
rows.source_section_or_page: Sizing table: WSFU 12-32 -> AA-B; 25 ft of 1 in branch is at/above the long-branch threshold so the placement note is the end-of-branch form

rows.source_title: Closed-system expansion-tank sizing identity: V_t = V_s * ((rho_cold/rho_hot) - 1) / (1 - P_i_abs/P_f_abs)
rows.source_edition_or_year: ASHRAE Systems & Equipment (water density 60 F / 200 F from NIST)
rows.source_section_or_page: 100 gal system, 60 F -> 200 F, 12 psig fill, 30 psig relief -> ~9.25 gal expansion-tank volume

rows.source_title: Drainfield area = design_flow_gpd / application_rate; trench length = area / trench_width
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; classical septic-system identity over state-published soil application rates
rows.source_section_or_page: 600 gpd design flow / 0.6 gpd/ft^2 application rate -> 1000 ft^2 required; with 3 ft trench width -> 333.3 lf of trench

rows.source_title: Sum loads, apply growth reserve, round up to ANSI standard step (15, 30, 45, 75, 112.5, 150, ...)
rows.source_edition_or_year: NEC 2023 Art. 450 sizing intent over ANSI/IEEE C57 standard kVA steps
rows.source_section_or_page: Loads {25 kVA, 18 kVA, 7500 W @ 0.85 pf = 8.82 kVA, 15 kVA} -> 66.82 kVA connected; +25% growth -> 83.53 kVA required; next ANSI step 112.5 kVA

rows.source_title: NEC 2023 Article 220 Part III standard service-load calculation
rows.source_section_or_page: 2500 ft^2 dwelling + 2 small-appliance + 1 laundry + 5 fixed @ 8000 W + 12 kW range + 5 kW dryer + 1.5 kW largest motor + 6 kW cooling vs 9 kW heating (heating governs) @ 240 V -> 34,525 VA / 143.85 A required; next standard breaker 150 A

rows.source_title: Compressible orifice low-dP flow: Q (cfh) = 3600 * c * A * sqrt(2 * dP / rho)
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; standard orifice flow identity, SG natural gas 0.60, c = 0.7
rows.source_section_or_page: 0.05 in orifice / 0.25 psi upstream natural gas -> ~3.15 cfh leak; orifice area ~0.00196 in^2

rows.source_title: Sensible heat ratio: SHR = sensible_load / total_load
rows.source_section_or_page: 24,000 BTU/hr sensible / 30,000 BTU/hr total -> SHR 0.80 (typical residential cooling)

rows.source_title: Equivalent length per fitting type and diameter, summed across the run
rows.source_edition_or_year: ASHRAE Handbook Fundamentals fitting-loss tables; SMACNA HVAC Systems Duct Design
rows.source_section_or_page: Four long-radius 90-degree elbows at 1 in (1.7 ft each) + one branch tee at 1 in (6 ft) -> 12.8 ft total equivalent length

rows.source_title: Slant/Fin Fine Line 30 Series technical bulletin: BTU/ft at design water temperature and 1 gpm flow
rows.source_section_or_page: 180 F water / 1 gpm / 8 ft of Slant/Fin Fine Line 30 -> 600 BTU/ft, 4800 BTU/hr total output

rows.source_title: Joukowsky surge: dP (psi) = rho * a * dV (Pa) / 6895 with elastic-coupling-adjusted celerity a
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; classical Joukowsky identity over manufacturer copper-tube elasticity (Type L 1 in)
rows.source_section_or_page: Copper Type L 1 in / water at 60 F / 8 fps velocity / 0.05 s closure / 100 ft run -> celerity ~4462 fps, surge ~481 psi, reflection time ~0.045 s (not rapid since closure > reflection)

rows.source_title: Watts Series 909 RP technical bulletin: typical pressure loss across reduced-pressure backflow assemblies
rows.source_edition_or_year: Manufacturer technical bulletin (typical RP-class loss across nominal sizes)
rows.source_section_or_page: 1 in RP at 30 gpm -> ~8.5 psi typical loss across the assembly

rows.source_title: Thermal expansion: dL = alpha * L * dT; expansion-loop leg L_loop = sqrt(3 * E * D * dL / sigma_allow)
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; classical pipe-thermal-expansion identity over A53 Grade B steel properties
rows.source_section_or_page: Carbon steel A53 4.5 in OD / 200 ft run / 100 F dT -> 1.56 in axial growth; ~18.73 ft loop leg

rows.source_title: Pump-curve / system-curve intersection: operating point where H_pump(Q) = H_static + k * Q^2
rows.source_edition_or_year: Engineering-practice composite end-suction centrifugal, 60 Hz (illustrative; replace with a manufacturer-attributed curve before selection)
rows.source_section_or_page: Static head 30 ft / friction k = 0.003 / small centrifugal -> intersects near 113 gpm at ~68 ft head, ~68% efficiency

rows.source_title: Recirculation pump head: friction loss over (pipe length + fittings equivalent length) at target flow
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; ASHRAE Handbook fitting equivalent-length tables over copper pipe (0.75 in ID)
rows.source_section_or_page: 100 ft of 0.75 in copper / 8 fittings (eq. ~16 ft) / 4 gpm target -> ~2.94 ft head / ~1.27 psi

rows.source_title: Geothermal ground-loop length: design_btu / soil-class BTU per ft (vertical or horizontal loop)
rows.source_edition_or_year: IGSHPA design guide; ASHRAE Applications GSHP chapter
rows.source_section_or_page: 60,000 BTU/hr heating (governs over 48,000 BTU/hr cooling) / clay soil / vertical loop @ 40 BTU/ft -> 1500 ft of bore

rows.source_title: Cumulative voltage drop along a feeder with discrete loads at distance
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; NEC 215 advisory + Ohm's-law identity over copper resistance per foot
rows.source_section_or_page: 12 AWG copper feeder at 120 V with 5 A @ 50 ft and 10 A @ 100 ft -> worst drop 4.05 V (3.37%) at the second load

rows.source_title: IEEE 802.3at (PoE+/Type 2) PSE budget vs. cable-loss derate over Cat6 run length
rows.source_section_or_page: Type 2 PSE = 30 W, PD min 25.5 W; 200 ft Cat6 @ 25 C -> 1.79 W cable loss, 28.21 W available at PD (green flag)

rows.source_title: Per-phase load summation + single-swap recommendation that minimizes imbalance over the mean
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; NEC 220 imbalance advisory + greedy single-swap heuristic
rows.source_section_or_page: Six-circuit panel skewed onto A (65 A) vs B (22 A) vs C (12 A) -> 160.6% imbalance; moving HVAC (30 A) A->C projects 60.6% imbalance

rows.source_title: Point-to-point short-circuit current at a downstream panel via the M-factor over cable C-value
rows.source_edition_or_year: Bussmann Selecting Protective Devices (point-to-point method); IEEE 1584 reference
rows.source_section_or_page: 1500 kVA / 5.75 %Z / 480 V three-phase utility -> 31,379 A at the secondary; 100 ft cable (C=22,185) -> 20,776 A at the downstream panel

rows.source_title: Bare-pipe vs insulated-pipe heat loss per ft (radial conduction + convective/radiative surface loss)
rows.source_edition_or_year: ASHRAE Fundamentals heat-transfer chapter; manufacturer fiberglass k = 0.025 W/m-K typical
rows.source_section_or_page: 2.375 in OD pipe at 200 F into 70 F still air with 1.5 in fiberglass -> 124.33 BTU/hr-ft bare vs 22.40 BTU/hr-ft insulated (82% effectiveness)

rows.source_title: NEC 2023 Article 220 demand-load calculation (simplified residential variant)
rows.source_section_or_page: 2000 ft^2 dwelling with 2 small-appliance + 1 laundry + 6 kW fixed + 12 kW range + 5 kW dryer; cooling 5 kW vs heating 8 kW (heating governs); @ 240 V -> 34,225 VA / 142.6 A required / 150 A next standard breaker

rows.source_title: Generator sizing: sum running watts; surge = max(starting + sum_other_running)
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; standard manufacturer-engineering identity over running + starting per-load watts
rows.source_section_or_page: Refrigerator (700 / 2200) + Lights (400 / 400) + Sump pump (800 / 2000) -> 1900 W running; 3400 W surge (sump-pump start governs)

rows.source_title: NEC 690.7 / 690.8 PV string sizing: Voc at record-low corrected by temperature coefficient + inverter MPPT window
rows.source_edition_or_year: NEC 2023 Art. 690 (Voc temperature correction; inverter MPPT vs Vdc max)
rows.source_section_or_page: Module 40 V Voc / 33 V Vmp / 0.3%/C at -10 C record low and 45 C record high; inverter 200/480 V MPPT, 600 V Vdc max -> max series 13 / min series 7; no flag

rows.source_title: Per-phase load sum + single-swap greedy that reduces imbalance toward the mean
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; NEC Art. 220 imbalance advisory + greedy swap heuristic
rows.source_section_or_page: Four circuits {A:1500, A:800, B:600, C:700} -> 141.67% initial imbalance; one swap (A->B of the 800 W circuit) -> 66.67% final imbalance

rows.source_title: Insulation thickness to hold outer surface temperature at limit: radial conduction with surface convection
rows.source_edition_or_year: ASHRAE Fundamentals heat-transfer chapter (radial conduction over pipe insulation)
rows.source_section_or_page: 1 in OD pipe at 250 F into 75 F ambient, 120 F surface limit, k=0.27 BTU in/hr-ft^2-F -> ~2.61 in thickness (r2 = 3.11 in)

rows.source_title: Psychrometric state at sea-level atmospheric pressure: dew point, GPP, vapor pressure from dry-bulb and RH
rows.source_edition_or_year: ASHRAE Fundamentals psychrometric chapter (Magnus saturation; 1013.25 hPa)
rows.source_section_or_page: 75 F @ 50% RH -> dew point ~55.1 F, ~64.5 GPP (grains per pound dry air); saturation pressure ~29.58 hPa

rows.source_title: Standing-water inventory: gallons = area_ft^2 * depth_in * 0.6234; weight = volume * 62.4 lb/ft^3
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; arithmetic identity over 7.48 gal/ft^3 and 62.4 lb/ft^3
rows.source_section_or_page: 500 ft^2 of standing water 1 in deep -> 41.67 ft^3 / 311.69 gal / 2600 lb

rows.source_title: Negative-air-machine sizing: required_cfm = room_volume_ft^3 * target_ACH / 60
rows.source_section_or_page: 8000 ft^3 chamber at 6 ACH -> 800 cfm required; one 1000-cfm or two 500-cfm units satisfy

rows.source_title: RH and dew point from dry-bulb / wet-bulb pair via the psychrometric wet-bulb identity
rows.source_section_or_page: 80 F dry-bulb / 67 F wet-bulb at 1013.25 hPa -> ~50.7% RH, ~60.1 F dew point, ~77.4 GPP

rows.source_title: Cable-pulling tension build-up: straight = m*L*mu*W; bend = T_in * e^(mu*theta); sidewall pressure check
rows.source_edition_or_year: Polywater / NECA cable-pulling guide (polymer-lubricated mu = 0.2 typical)
rows.source_section_or_page: 1.5 lb/ft cable / 100 ft straight / one 90-deg bend at 2 ft radius / polymer lube -> 71.07 lb tension, 20.54 lb/ft sidewall (both ok flags)

rows.source_title: Elevation head: P (psi) = h (ft) * rho (lb/ft^3) / 144
rows.source_edition_or_year: n/a; arithmetic identity from water density 62.4 lb/ft^3
rows.source_section_or_page: 30 ft column of water -> 30 * 62.4 / 144 = 13.00 psi elevation; plus 5 psi friction -> 18.00 psi total
